name: Myristica fragrans Houtt.
intensity: Mild to moderate (depends heavily on dose)
mechanism: myristicin metabolizes into mmda-like compounds affecting serotonin and dopamine systems | anticholinergic and hallucinogenic effects
contraindications: avoid with hepatotoxic in large doses
interactions: Avoid with: Increases effects of sedatives and antidepressants
May potentiate: interacts with liver enzymes
safetyNotes: Toxic in large amounts,high doses may cause liver damage,delirium,or seizures,Nausea,vomiting,dry mouth,palpitations,hallucinations,confusion,liver toxicity,Hepatotoxic at high doses,myristicin is neurotoxic in excess,Myristicin LD50 in rats: ~400–700 mg/kg oral,human toxic dose ~5g+
description: Myristica fragrans Houtt. is a medicinal herb traditionally used for euphoria and hallucinations.
legalStatus: Legal globally; psychoactive use discouraged or regulated in prisons/schools
